seoTitle: Devonport vs Doctors Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Devonport and Doctors Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Devonport vs Doctors Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Devonport and Doctors Point.
heading: Devonport vs Doctors Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Devonport, Tasmania and Doctors Point, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Devonport has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Doctors Point (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Devonport or Doctors Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Devonport has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Doctors Point scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Devonport scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Doctors Point scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Devonport or Doctors Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Devonport has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
